---
schema: verdi.deviation/v1
covers: 024b2458f86204bcd7f3e792893cb38b8bc2612d
findings:
  - { id: judged-vl022-wrong-class-target-skipped-vs-ac3-letter, kind: judged, text: "Persists at HEAD (024b245): AC-3's frozen text promises 'wrong class' is one of the refusals 'each named with the offending value', and the spec body's AC-3 lists 'the resolved target's class is not story' as a refused shape — but internal/lint/vl022.go's badAttestationVerifiesTarget returns no finding when a verifies edge resolves to a non-story spec (target.Class != ClassStory → return \"\", false), skipping it per Controller adjudication ADJ-51's rescope of VL-022 to story-targeting attestations (dc-4's empirical premise that no stored attestation carries a verifies edge was false against 11 frozen R4-I-11 feature-outcome attestations, and refusing them broke make verify). The frozen ac-3/dc-4 text was never revised through ratification, so the contract-vs-behavior gap remains semantic: an attestation sitting at a story's fold path whose id agrees with its path (VL-011 passes) but whose verifies edge names a class: feature spec passes VL-022 with zero findings, and once authored (marker removed) folds as evidence for the story AC via LoadAttestationState, which never reads the verifies edge — a wrong-class variant of the D6-18 silent-fold class AC-3 exists to kill. Already dispositioned accepted-deviation in this spec's deviation report (ADJ-51, STOP 1); re-raised because the deviation is still present in the shipped rule and the frozen spec text still promises otherwise. (confidence 0.75)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Standing class per ADJ-51 (STOP 1): VL-022 is rescoped to story-targeting attestations because dc-4's no-verifies-edge premise was empirically false against 11 frozen R4-I-11 feature-outcome attestations; refusing them broke make verify. The frozen ac-3/dc-4 text is intentionally not edited (frozen specs ride the ratification flow, not a build-time edit); this disposition is the store-resident record of that ruling. Per ADJ-59, this re-records the standing adjudication on the regenerated text." }
  - { id: judged-resolution-seam-feature-shadowing-and-multi-match-third-verdict-case, kind: judged, text: "Persists at HEAD: AC-2's body pins resolution to 'the shared two-form contract (I-30, internal/storyresolve.Resolve)' but cmd/verdi/attest.go's classifyPair resolves via buildstart.go's resolveBuildTarget, whose class: story fallback engages only when Resolve fails with 'no active feature spec has story'. Two semantic divergences from the spec's letter follow: (a) a class: feature spec sharing the story's tracker ref always wins scheme-prefixed resolution (the fallback never runs), so a story ref that genuinely names a declared (story, AC) pair — AC-1's primary precondition — is refused exit 1 as 'resolves to a feature-class spec, not a story', leaving that pair reachable only via the spec-ref spelling; (b) a story ref matching more than one active class: story spec maps to an exit-1 refusal — a third verdict case AC-2's 'exactly two cases' never enumerates, for a pair that exists (twice) rather than 'does not exist'. Already dispositioned accepted-deviation (ADJ-51 finding 2: the contract cited a seam whose story-ref path is permanently feature-class-only by its own doc comment, so the build reused the correct real seam verdi build start established); re-raised because the frozen ac-2 text and shipped behavior still disagree pending ratification. (confidence 0.50)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Standing class per ADJ-51 (finding 2): the frozen contract cited a seam whose scheme-prefixed story-ref path is permanently feature-class-only by its own doc comment, so the build correctly reuses the real resolveBuildTarget seam verdi build start already established, disclosed rather than silently substituted. Per ADJ-59, this re-records the standing adjudication on the regenerated text." }
digest: sha256:a981d8f1238852867830eddcd51cd951f23baeaad91629ba9223c372ae5514da
integrity: sha256:e8d1582b0c39f5121f3df1e8975c6be0ee435b9468bf835417d1effa31a5c2f2
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "```json\n{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"vl022-wrong-class-target-skipped-vs-ac3-letter\",\"text\":\"Persists at HEAD (024b245): AC-3's frozen text promises 'wrong class' is one of the refusals 'each named with the offending value', and the spec body's AC-3 lists 'the resolved target's class is not story' as a refused shape — but internal/lint/vl022.go's badAttestationVerifiesTarget returns no finding when a verifies edge resolves to a non-story spec (target.Class != ClassStory → return \\\"\\\", false), skipping it per Controller adjudication ADJ-51's rescope of VL-022 to story-targeting attestations (dc-4's empirical premise that no stored attestation carries a verifies edge was false against 11 frozen R4-I-11 feature-outcome attestations, and refusing them broke make verify). The frozen ac-3/dc-4 text was never revised through ratification, so the contract-vs-behavior gap remains semantic: an attestation sitting at a story's fold path whose id agrees with its path (VL-011 passes) but whose verifies edge names a class: feature spec passes VL-022 with zero findings, and once authored (marker removed) folds as evidence for the story AC via LoadAttestationState, which never reads the verifies edge — a wrong-class variant of the D6-18 silent-fold class AC-3 exists to kill. Already dispositioned accepted-deviation in this spec's deviation report (ADJ-51, STOP 1); re-raised because the deviation is still present in the shipped rule and the frozen spec text still promises otherwise.\",\"confidence\":0.75},{\"id\":\"resolution-seam-feature-shadowing-and-multi-match-third-verdict-case\",\"text\":\"Persists at HEAD: AC-2's body pins resolution to 'the shared two-form contract (I-30, internal/storyresolve.Resolve)' but cmd/verdi/attest.go's classifyPair resolves via buildstart.go's resolveBuildTarget, whose class: story fallback engages only when Resolve fails with 'no active feature spec has story'. Two semantic divergences from the spec's letter follow: (a) a class: feature spec sharing the story's tracker ref always wins scheme-prefixed resolution (the fallback never runs), so a story ref that genuinely names a declared (story, AC) pair — AC-1's primary precondition — is refused exit 1 as 'resolves to a feature-class spec, not a story', leaving that pair reachable only via the spec-ref spelling; (b) a story ref matching more than one active class: story spec maps to an exit-1 refusal — a third verdict case AC-2's 'exactly two cases' never enumerates, for a pair that exists (twice) rather than 'does not exist'. Already dispositioned accepted-deviation (ADJ-51 finding 2: the contract cited a seam whose story-ref path is permanently feature-class-only by its own doc comment, so the build reused the correct real seam verdi build start established); re-raised because the frozen ac-2 text and shipped behavior still disagree pending ratification.\",\"confidence\":0.5}]}\n```" }
frozen: { at: 2026-07-17, commit: 024b2458f86204bcd7f3e792893cb38b8bc2612d }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v0, inputs: [spec/attest-helper@024b2458f86204bcd7f3e792893cb38b8bc2612d, spec/attest-helper@e606a109dbc28ea08cc86265c4fa2dd026f8373a], digest: sha256:a981d8f1238852867830eddcd51cd951f23baeaad91629ba9223c372ae5514da, integrity: sha256:e8d1582b0c39f5121f3df1e8975c6be0ee435b9468bf835417d1effa31a5c2f2 }
---
# Alignment report

## Computed

(none)

### Boundary diff vs acceptance baseline

(no impacted services)

### Diagram alignment

- (no accepted proposals)
- (no illustrative diagrams in this spec's body)

## Judged

- **judged-vl022-wrong-class-target-skipped-vs-ac3-letter** [accepted-deviation]: Persists at HEAD (024b245): AC-3's frozen text promises 'wrong class' is one of the refusals 'each named with the offending value', and the spec body's AC-3 lists 'the resolved target's class is not story' as a refused shape — but internal/lint/vl022.go's badAttestationVerifiesTarget returns no finding when a verifies edge resolves to a non-story spec (target.Class != ClassStory → return "", false), skipping it per Controller adjudication ADJ-51's rescope of VL-022 to story-targeting attestations (dc-4's empirical premise that no stored attestation carries a verifies edge was false against 11 frozen R4-I-11 feature-outcome attestations, and refusing them broke make verify). The frozen ac-3/dc-4 text was never revised through ratification, so the contract-vs-behavior gap remains semantic: an attestation sitting at a story's fold path whose id agrees with its path (VL-011 passes) but whose verifies edge names a class: feature spec passes VL-022 with zero findings, and once authored (marker removed) folds as evidence for the story AC via LoadAttestationState, which never reads the verifies edge — a wrong-class variant of the D6-18 silent-fold class AC-3 exists to kill. Already dispositioned accepted-deviation in this spec's deviation report (ADJ-51, STOP 1); re-raised because the deviation is still present in the shipped rule and the frozen spec text still promises otherwise. (confidence 0.75) — Standing class per ADJ-51 (STOP 1): VL-022 is rescoped to story-targeting attestations because dc-4's no-verifies-edge premise was empirically false against 11 frozen R4-I-11 feature-outcome attestations; refusing them broke make verify. The frozen ac-3/dc-4 text is intentionally not edited (frozen specs ride the ratification flow, not a build-time edit); this disposition is the store-resident record of that ruling. Per ADJ-59, this re-records the standing adjudication on the regenerated text.
- **judged-resolution-seam-feature-shadowing-and-multi-match-third-verdict-case** [accepted-deviation]: Persists at HEAD: AC-2's body pins resolution to 'the shared two-form contract (I-30, internal/storyresolve.Resolve)' but cmd/verdi/attest.go's classifyPair resolves via buildstart.go's resolveBuildTarget, whose class: story fallback engages only when Resolve fails with 'no active feature spec has story'. Two semantic divergences from the spec's letter follow: (a) a class: feature spec sharing the story's tracker ref always wins scheme-prefixed resolution (the fallback never runs), so a story ref that genuinely names a declared (story, AC) pair — AC-1's primary precondition — is refused exit 1 as 'resolves to a feature-class spec, not a story', leaving that pair reachable only via the spec-ref spelling; (b) a story ref matching more than one active class: story spec maps to an exit-1 refusal — a third verdict case AC-2's 'exactly two cases' never enumerates, for a pair that exists (twice) rather than 'does not exist'. Already dispositioned accepted-deviation (ADJ-51 finding 2: the contract cited a seam whose story-ref path is permanently feature-class-only by its own doc comment, so the build reused the correct real seam verdi build start established); re-raised because the frozen ac-2 text and shipped behavior still disagree pending ratification. (confidence 0.50) — Standing class per ADJ-51 (finding 2): the frozen contract cited a seam whose scheme-prefixed story-ref path is permanently feature-class-only by its own doc comment, so the build correctly reuses the real resolveBuildTarget seam verdi build start already established, disclosed rather than silently substituted. Per ADJ-59, this re-records the standing adjudication on the regenerated text.
